title: Evoked responses to elevated sounds
description: This dataset comprises neurophysiological recordings from two experiments investigating auditory spatial processing and sound localization. Subjects were presented with noise bursts from loudspeakers positioned at various elevations while their evoked responses were recorded. The first experiment employed a deviant detection paradigm with adapter-probe stimulus pairs, while the second used a one-back task with headphone-delivered stimuli and loudspeaker-based targets. A subset of participants in the second experiment was tested with ear canal molds to disrupt elevation perception.
methods_description: Data were collected in a hemi-anechoic chamber. Experiment 1 (Deviant Detection) presented adapter noise bursts followed by probe bursts from loudspeakers at different elevations (±37.5°, ±12.5°, -12.5°). Experiment 2 (One-Back) delivered white noise through open headphones followed by noise bursts from loudspeakers at elevations of ±37.5°, ±12.5°, and -23.5°. Approximately 5% of trials in Experiment 2 were targets requiring behavioral localization responses. A subset of Experiment 2 participants (sub-130-134) completed a second session with ear canal molds inserted.
